name: Chihuahua (Mexico)
description: A white field with the coat of arms of the state of Chihuahua centred.
history: Chihuahua has no officially adopted state flag; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.

The arms feature elements recalling the founding of the city of Chihuahua in 1709, the Sierra Madre Occidental, the mining and agricultural traditions of the state, and its place on the northern frontier of Mexico.
